chunk_summary: This section focuses on food insecurity screening and adapting meal plans to budget tiers during clinic safety triage and initial assessment, emphasizing non-stigmatizing approaches, tiered dietary plans, and linkage to support services like ICDS and PM POSHAN.
learning_objectives: Understand how to screen for food insecurity using a validated two-item tool.
Learn to adapt dietary counseling to budget tiers for families facing food insecurity.
Identify and link families to local nutrition support services like ICDS and PM POSHAN.
Apply motivational interviewing techniques to collaboratively set realistic dietary goals.
key_facts: Food insecurity screening uses two questions: worry about food running out and food not lasting with no money for more.
Tiered meal plans: Tier 1 focuses on quality; Tier 2 protects protein and structure; Tier 3 prioritizes safety and calories.
Low-cost protein options include dal, rajma, chana, eggs, soya nuggets, peanuts, and til.
Linkage to services includes ICDS for under-6s, PM POSHAN for school meals, and PDS/ration card support.
Document screening results in one sentence: 'Food insecurity screen positive; plan adapted to budget tier; linkage discussed.'
reference_quote: Development and validity of a 2-item screen to identify families at risk for food insecurity — 'Because food costs are rising, I ask all families two routine questions.'
